channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ASX200 falls as oil supply fears rise amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Investor concerns over the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran, coupled with heightened fears about global oil supply, have pushed the Australian share market sharply lower. The S&P/ASX200 fell 1.3 ...

item.title: Yanga defender Dickson Job to miss three months due to knee surgery
item.description: Dar es Salaam: Young Africans (Yanga) and Taifa Stars defender, Dickson Job, has successfully undergone knee surgery in South Africa today, March 11, 2026, following an injury he got during the Kariak...

item.title: Chinese and Indian Carmakers Gain Ground in South Africa’s Auto Market
item.description: South Africa's automotive sector is experiencing a notable shift as Chinese and Indian carmakers expand their presence. Budget-friendly brands like Chery and Great Wall Motor have seen increased marke...

item.title: Poilievre to Visit U.S. to Highlight Canadian Trade, Auto, and Energy Interests
item.description: Federal Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is embarking on a U.S. tour next week to promote Canada's auto and energy sectors and to advocate for tariff-free trade. The trip includes stops in Michiga...

item.title: South Africa summons US ambassador over critical remarks on policies and judiciary
item.description: South Africa has formally summoned the United States ambassador, Leo Brent Bozell III, after he made comments criticising several of the country's policies and questioning the authority of its courts....

item.title: Valverde Shines as Real Madrid Beat Man City; PSG Overpower Chelsea in UCL
item.description: For this Wednesday for UEFA Champions League Round of 16 first-leg, Real Madrid dey show say dem no dey joke as dem beat Manchester City 3-0. The highlight of the match na Real Madrid captain Federico...

item.title: Sonko Rescue Team Helps Families of Nairobi Flood Victims with Burials
item.description: On Wednesday, March 11, Nairobi saw an emotional moment as the Sonko Rescue Team transported five of the twenty-seven victims of the recent floods to their homes for burial. The victims were taken to ...

item.title: Study reveals racial biases in praise for black and white female footballers in 2019 Women's World Cup commentary
item.description: A 2025 study analysing BBC and ITV commentary from the 2019 FIFA Women's World Cup has uncovered significant racial disparities in how praise is distributed to female footballers. Researchers examined...

item.title: BJP Criticises Rahul Gandhi Over Speaker Removal Motion
item.description: In a heated session of the Lok Sabha, governing NDA MPs strongly criticised Rahul Gandhi, accusing him of attempting to destabilise parliamentary procedures with the recent motion seeking the removal ...

item.title: FG maintains market-driven petrol pricing amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The Federal Government don yan say e no dey plan to control petrol price, even as wahala dey Middle East wey dey affect global oil market. Wale Edun, Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of t...

item.title: South Carolina Measles Outbreak Shows Downward Trend After Reaching Nearly 1,000 Cases
item.description: A significant measles outbreak in South Carolina, the largest in the United States in over 30 years, is demonstrating signs of slowing after peaking in mid-January. The state has reported a steady dec...

item.title: Learning Today’s Filipino Word: Gasolina
item.description: ABS-CBN Lifestyle introduces its Word of the Day for March 12, 2026: 'gasolina.' This feature aims to engage Filipino audiences in language learning by highlighting a single word each day and providin...

item.title: Most Charges Dropped in Canadian Anti-Israel Protest Cases
item.description: Since the escalation of conflict in Israel in October 2023, numerous anti-Israel protests in Canada have led to arrests, yet the majority of resulting criminal charges have been dropped, stayed, or ot...

item.title: Government documents reveal Starmer was informed of Mandelson's close Epstein ties prior to US ambassador appointment
item.description: Newly released government documents show that Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer was explicitly warned about Lord Peter Mandelson's longstanding and close relationship with the convicted sex offender Jef...

item.title: Kabuu's ex‑husband appeals Sh70 million matrimonial property ruling in Nairobi High Court
item.description: Marathon star Lucy Kabuu’s ex‑husband, Jeremiah Maina Wamungu, has appealed a Nairobi High Court decision that awarded her almost the entire Sh70 million matrimonial estate the two had accumulated dur...

item.title: Cape Town's Chief Heat Officer addresses extreme heat challenges during current heatwave
item.description: Cape Town is currently experiencing a severe heatwave, with temperatures soaring into the high 30s and low 40s across parts of the Western Cape. This has led to increased vegetation fires, crowded bea...

item.title: CCTV Shows Man Stealing from Nairobi Woman-Owned Shop
item.description: In Nairobi along Luthuli Avenue, a CCTV camera captured a disturbing incident inside a woman-owned business shop. A man visited the shop pretending to be interested in buying products, but he had a hi...

item.title: China Strengthens Partnership with Tanzania to Boost Local Manufacturing and Skills Development
item.description: China has confirmed its strong support to Tanzania in boosting the country’s industrial sector, focusing on manufacturing, technology transfer, and skills development. During her visit to Saturn Corpo...

item.title: Lok Sabha rejects Opposition's no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla
item.description: In a significant development in Parliament, the Lok Sabha on Wednesday rejected the Opposition's motion to remove Speaker Om Birla through a voice vote. The motion, brought by the Congress party, accu...

item.title: Panchayati Raj Ministry Introduces 'Nirbhay Raho' to Strengthen Safety and Leadership of Women in Rural Areas
item.description: The Ministry of Panchayati Raj convened a national conference on Wednesday with over 700 participants, mostly women, to mark one year of the 'Sashakt Panchayat - Netri Abhiyan' programme. During the e...
